items.prompt: Decide whether the table `(2, 6), (4, 12), (7, 21)` is proportional and justify the constant of proportionality.
items.expected_evidence: explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the unit rate for 18 miles in 3 hours and explain what it means.
items.expected_evidence: find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find 15% of 80 and explain how the percent is acting as a rate per hundred.
items.expected_evidence: compare two situations by coordinating the same unit basis
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compare two situations by coordinating the same unit basis with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why equal differences alone do not prove a relationship is proportional.
items.expected_evidence: explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit.

items.prompt: Explain how a unit rate and a constant of proportionality describe the same multiplicative structure.
items.expected_evidence: find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks.

items.prompt: A student says a table is proportional because it increases by 3 each row. Diagnose the additive-pattern error. Focus on total quantity fixation.
items.expected_evidence: explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit.

items.prompt: A student scales one quantity by 2 but the other by 3 and still claims proportionality. Identify the breakdown. Focus on division structure breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks.

items.prompt: A map uses 1 cm for every 5 km. How far does 7.2 cm represent, and why is the relationship proportional?
items.expected_evidence: explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A recipe uses 3 cups of flour for 2 batches. Write an equation relating flour to batches and find the amount for 5 batches.
items.expected_evidence: find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves find unit rate in bounded ratio and measurement tasks in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, determine whether 4 notebooks for $6 and 10 notebooks for $15 are proportional and justify your reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates explain why miles per hour or cost per item describes an amount for one unit on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Probe ratio as multiplicative comparison before diagnosing unit rate reasoning, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether ratio as multiplicative comparison is secure enough to support unit rate reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in ratio as multiplicative comparison from a current-node issue in unit rate reasoning.

items.prompt: Probe unit conversion within measurement systems before diagnosing unit rate reasoning, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether unit conversion within measurement systems is secure enough to support unit rate reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in unit conversion within measurement systems from a current-node issue in unit rate reasoning.
